description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages build, pre-release, release, and deploy using the node:17, electronuserland/builder, electronuserland/builder:wine, and snapcore/snapcraft:stable Docker images and the docker runner tag. In the build stage add a build job that runs on all branches except master and tags, uses node:17, and executes `npm ci`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run webpack`. Also in the build stage add a package-linux-snap-test job that runs on branches other than master and tags, uses electronuserland/builder, and runs `npm ci`, `npm run webpack`, then `npm run package-linux-snap`. In the release stage add package-linux-snap, package-linux-targz, package-mac-targz, package-win-nsis, and package-win-zip jobs that each run only on tags, use a shared before_script to replace the version placeholder in package.json with $CI_COMMIT_TAG, run `npm ci` and `npm run webpack` (the win jobs use the wine image), then run their respective `npm run package-*` command, move the generated *.snap, *.tar.gz, *.exe, or *.zip file to the project root, and publish it as an artifact named ${CI_PROJECT_NAME}_${CI_COMMIT_TAG} with the matching path. Add a pages job in the deploy stage that runs on tags, uses node:17, runs `sed -i package.json -e "s#0\.0\.0-tag#$CI_COMMIT_TAG#g"`, `npm ci`, and `npm run public`, and publishes the public/ directory as a pages artifact. Finally, add a publish-linux-snap job in deploy that depends on package-linux-snap, runs on tags with snapcore/snapcraft:stable, decodes $SNAPCRAFT_LOGIN_FILE, logs into snapcraft, and pushes the *.snap with `snapcraft push *.snap --release beta`, and a release job that runs on the master branch (excluding tags) with node:17 to execute `npx semantic-release-gitlab`.
